title: Iran Threatens OpenAI's UAE Data Center
summary: Iran's IRGC has threatened OpenAI's Abu Dhabi data center in response to potential US attacks on Iranian power plants. The threat includes destruction of US-linked energy and tech companies in the region.
body:
Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) has published a video threatening OpenAI's planned Abu Dhabi data center. The threat is in response to potential US attacks on Iranian power plants. The video, published on an Iranian state-backed news outlet's X account, warns of the "complete and utter annihilation" of US-linked energy and technology companies in the region.

 The threat against OpenAI's data center is significant, given the company's substantial investment in the region. The $30 billion Stargate data center in Abu Dhabi is a major undertaking for OpenAI, and its destruction would have considerable implications for the company's operations and the broader tech industry. The IRGC's threat also highlights the geopolitical tensions in the region and the potential risks faced by US-linked companies.

 The situation is being closely watched, with many awaiting a response from OpenAI and the US government. The threat has raised concerns about the safety and security of tech infrastructure in the region, and it remains to be seen how the situation will unfold. Reactions from the international community and the potential consequences for Iran's relations with the US and other countries are also being closely monitored.
